text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously constructive. The real driver today is China's faster-than-expected factory growth on tech export demand - that's direct fuel for chip and AI names like SMH and SOXL. Iran deal removing near-term geopolitical risk is also lifting sentiment broadly. Your kind of names get a tailwind here.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the main concern - it's a 3x leveraged ETF that decays daily; on margin that's a double-whammy if chips stall. Stop is $200, price $236, so you have room but watch it closely. SPCX (new) is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with thin data - margin amplifies any gap-down hard. SMH (-0.69%) is minor but worth watching given leverage exposure.

Watch: 1) Semis reaction to China factory data - does SMH break higher or stall? 2) Yen at 40-year low; if intervention hits, risk-off could slam leveraged positions fast.

Bottom line: Your leveraged and new-IPO positions are the live wires - keep stops honest and don't add size until SOXL stabilizes.
plays: ALAB long - scanner's highest-scoring new idea (8/12, R 4.38); consider entry in the $446.42-$447.54 zone, stop $442.64, targets start at $468.97. Not a current holding, good diversification within yo

CRWD add - scanner shows ACCUMULATE 8/12 on a name you already own with a 3.26% gain; entry $741.42-$742.17, stop $740.31 (tight - honor it), targets $747.61-$759.22. Small add only, margin account me

SOXL - review your stop; it's your biggest loser at -7.06% on a 3x leveraged product. If it can't hold above $220 intraday on this positive China news, consider trimming to reduce margin exposure befo
